text.title: Internal Link Audit Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every internal-link audit must enforce. These prevent the most common SEO failure: a cluster strategy on paper that lacks linking glue in reality.

rule.statement: Every cluster page links to its pillar page using exact-or-partial-match anchor. Every pillar page links to ALL its cluster members in a "related articles" section or contextual paragraph. Reject clusters with < 80% cluster-to-pillar coverage OR < 80% pillar-to-cluster coverage.
rule.rationale: Search engines infer cluster cohesion from internal-link adjacency. Missing pillar-cluster links break the cohesion signal.

rule.statement: No published content page has zero incoming internal links. Pages with 0 incoming links are added to the rewire plan with an explicit decision: link from ≥ 2 contextually relevant pages OR redirect/unpublish.
rule.rationale: Orphan pages are deprioritized in crawl scheduling and lose ranking despite content quality. The cost of fixing orphans is small; the SEO upside is large.

rule.statement: Per cluster, anchor-text distribution targets: exact-match ≤ 30%, partial-match 30-60%, generic / branded 10-20%. Reject clusters with exact-match anchor > 40% (over-optimization signal).
rule.rationale: Over-optimized anchors trigger algorithmic dampening. Diverse anchors look natural and pass more equity.

rule.statement: Reject hubs with > 200 incoming internal links unless the hub is intentionally a navigation root. Over-linked hubs distribute equity dilutely; trim or restructure.
rule.rationale: Internal PageRank-equivalent dilution sets in past ~ 200 links; trimming improves per-cluster page authority.

rule.statement: Every rewire plan declares ≥ 3 baseline metrics (cluster impressions, cluster avg position, cluster CTR) measured 90 days BEFORE the rewire, and commits to re-measurement 60+ days AFTER. Reject rewire plans without explicit before/after metrics.
rule.rationale: Without before/after measurement, the rewire is unattributable. The team can't tell what moved the needle and can't iterate.

example.title: Cluster with healthy adjacency
example.code:
Cluster: "JTBD" Pillar: /blog/jobs-to-be-done-guide Members: /blog/jtbd-interviews, /blog/jtbd-forces, /blog/jtbd-vs-personas, /blog/jtbd-examples, /blog/jtbd-templates
Adjacency: Cluster → Pillar: 5 / 5 links present (100%) ✅ Pillar → Cluster: 5 / 5 reciprocated (100%) ✅ Anchor mix: exact 22%, partial 51%, branded 18%, generic 9% ✅ Over-linked hubs: none (max page = 38 incoming) ✅
example.note: Illustrates r1, r3, r4 in a healthy cluster.

example.title: Orphan-heavy site with no cluster glue
example.code:
Site stats: Total pages: 62 Orphans (0 incoming): 14 (23%) Cluster-to-pillar: 12 of 28 (43%) Exact-match anchors: 71% in one cluster Hub overload: /resources page receives 412 internal links
Diagnosis: cluster strategy exists on paper, internal linking does not reinforce it. Orphans waste content investment.
example.note: Illustrates r1-r4 violations simultaneously — typical "audit-needed" state.
